message:
fix(server): add startup readiness self-probe and cap supervisor restart attempts

Address the macOS LaunchAgent bug where the process reports state=running
with a live PID but no server is actually reachable on the configured port.

Two complementary defenses:

1. Startup readiness self-probe: after binding the TCP listener, the
   supervisor now hits /healthz in a retry loop (250ms interval, 10s
   timeout) to confirm the server can actually serve requests.  If the
   probe fails, the server instance is stopped and the process exits so
   the service manager can restart it cleanly.  This catches silent
   failures during SQLite WAL recovery or first-run migrations.

2. Max consecutive restart failures: the respawn loop now tracks
   consecutive spawn failures and gives up after 10 attempts (~30s with
   exponential backoff 200ms-5s).  Previously a persistent bind failure
   would keep the process alive indefinitely in a degraded state.

Both mechanisms ensure the process exits promptly when it cannot serve
traffic, letting launchd/systemd perform a clean restart rather than
masking a stuck server behind a healthy-looking PID.

Includes a unit test that verifies the respawn loop gives up after
exactly HTTP_SUPERVISOR_MAX_CONSECUTIVE_RESTART_FAILURES attempts with
the correct error message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
